content:
Amy Gilliland is an Assistant Mapping Geologist with the Washington Geological Survey. In the field, her responsibilities include assisting the lead geologist with geologic data collection, geologic landscape interpretation, and geophysical data acquisition in Kittitas County, WA as part of the STATEMAP program.
Amy is also a graduate student with Central Washington University’s Geological Science Department. Her research involves investigating the spatial variations of surface morphology in the Transantarctic Mountains, Antarctica to improve scientific understanding of climate and tectonic events. She expects to graduate with her M.S. in Winter 2021.
In her free time, Amy enjoys snowboarding, cross-country skiing, hiking, mountain biking, cooking, and coding for fun.
summary: Amy Gilliland is an Assistant Mapping Geologist with the Washington Geological Survey. In the field, her responsibilities include assisting the lead geologist with geologic data collection, geologic landscape interpretation, and geophysical data acquisition in Kittitas County, WA as part of the STATEMAP program.
title: Amy Gilliland

content:
Fall quarter of 2018 I was a student in CWU’s field geodetics course taught by Dr. Tim Melbourne, Director of the Pacific Northwest Geodetic Array (PANGA). To kick off the class, we embarked on a trip to South Sister Volcano, Oregon from September 15th – 18th. During this trip, we backpacked with precision GPS equipment to known benchmarks, then left the GPS receivers to collect data for one day. We endured freezing temperatures and even snow during our trip, however, the beautiful scenery made the chilly weather worth it. On the final day of our trip, we retrieved the GPS receivers and departed South Sister Volcano.
  South Sister Volcano, Oregon   The remainder of the course was spent downloading, processing, and modeling our measurements and comparing to previous years, to image the volcanic inflationary signal and estimate the rate of filing of the magma chamber under South Sister Volcano.
     #
title: Geodesy of South Sister Volcano, OR
Paleoseismic survey of Whidbey Island, WA
UAV Survey of Summer Lake, OR
